manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: housewifery, defenseman, inflexibility, canola, candlepower, cattiness, playact, battleaxe, assassinator.
Your opponent's team words are: dwarfism, seafloor, plantain, decisiveness, multidimensionality, dangerousness, undernourishment, pleasantness.
Distractor words are: pockmark, gingersnap, ineptness, forestation, hyperthermia, undulation, cauterization.
The kill words are: towhead.

message.reasoning:
We need to provide a single-word clue relating to some of our team's words. Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts. Must be short, format.

Our words: housewifery, defensemen, inflexibility, canola, candlepower, cattiness, playact, battleaxe, assassinator.

We want to clue as many as possible but must be safe from opponent or distractor. Choose a clue that connects perhaps "war" for battleaxe, assassin, defenseman? "Combat" maybe relates to battleaxe, assassin, defenseman. Also "weapon"? Not in list. "Blade" maybe? Battleaxe, assassin (weapon). Defenseman uses stick? Not blade. Candlepower is about light. Could clue "light" for candlepower, canola (oil used for cooking/lighting?), not sure. Inflexibility maybe "rigid". Housewifery maybe "domestic". Cattiness maybe "snark". Playact maybe "drama". Could we clue "role"? Playact, assassinator (role), defenseman (role), housewifery (role). But risk? Probably safe.

We need single-word clue, not containing any parts of words. "Role" is a word but not part of any listed. Role relates to playact (role playing), defenseman (role in hockey), assassin (role), housewifery (role). Also battleaxe? Not role.

Maybe we aim for 4 words: defenseman, battleaxe, assassinator, playact: clue "combat"? combat relates: battleaxe (weapon), assassinator (combat), defenseman (defends), playact (act like fighting). Candlepower not.

Another could be "stubborn": inflexibility, cattiness? Not exactly.

Maybe "sharp": battleaxe, assassinator, candlepower (sharp light), canola (sharp oil?) not.

Could clue "oil": canola, candle (oil lamp?), battleaxe? no.

Better to pick a clue that clearly maps only to our words, avoid opponent/distractor. Let's consider clue "hockey". Defenseman is a hockey position. Battleaxe? Not. Assassin? Not. playact? Not. Not good.

Clue "axe": battleaxe, assassin (uses axe?), defenseman (uses stick) but not. Candlepower? no.

Clue "light": candlepower, canola (used to make biodiesel, maybe not light), housewifery (light house?) no.

Clue "domestic": housewifery, canola (grown domestically), maybe cattiness (domestic cat?), playact (domestic drama). Might be ambiguous but okay.

But need to give targets list.

We can choose to target perhaps 3: defenseman, battleaxe, assassinator -> clue "warrior". Warrior relates to battleaxe (weapon), defenseman (warrior in defense) and assassinator (warrior). Also playact (warrior role) maybe.

Is "warrior" acceptable? Not any list words. Good.

But does "warrior" risk opponent? Not.

Thus:

CLUE: warrior
TARGETS: defenseman, battleaxe, assassinator

Make sure format exactly
